title: Requirements, ER Modeling, Functional Dependencies, and Normalization · Result-Grain Case: Lossless Dependency Design
summary: A growing incident system repeats team and member facts, mixes multiple values per cell, and produces update anomalies. Planned artifact: Add and verify the sql lossless dependency design increment in a normalized incident-system schema defense. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Defend decomposition with lossless-join and dependency-preservation evidence and justify bounded denormalization.
Transform repeating or partially dependent data through 1NF, 2NF, 3NF, and BCNF reasoning.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.sql:
-- sql-basics cumulative relational data portfolio.
-- Each increment declares grain, predicts cardinality or changed rows, runs in fresh browser SQLite,
-- reconciles results, tests changed data, and records PostgreSQL or standard-SQL boundaries.

SELECT sqlite_version() AS browser_sqlite_version;

content.code:
-- Worked focus: sql-lossless-dependency-design — Defend decomposition with lossless-join and dependency-preservation evidence and justify bounded denormalization.
SELECT ticket_id, status, opened_at
FROM tickets
WHERE status <> 'resolved'
ORDER BY opened_at, ticket_id;
-- Record SQLite behavior, then verify syntax and semantics against PostgreSQL 18.4.

steps.title: Build Lossless Dependency Design evidence · Theory Lossless Dependency Design
steps.instruction:
Append a runnable SQLite increment headed "-- Evidence: sql-lossless-dependency-design-5ic1re" with a concrete grain, hypothesis, or prediction; schema grain, relationship, dependency, or decomposition evidence; and an observation, reconciliation, or verification tied to returned rows, changed rows, an error, or a plan.

content.code:
-- Evidence: sql-lossless-dependency-design-5ic1re
-- grain: declare the exact row or mutation grain before running
CREATE TABLE model_status_history_5ic1re (
  ticket_id INTEGER NOT NULL REFERENCES tickets(ticket_id),
  changed_at TEXT NOT NULL,
  status TEXT NOT NULL,
  PRIMARY KEY (ticket_id, changed_at)
);
-- observation: reconcile the returned rows, changed count, error, or plan with the prediction
